question: Burning 16 kg of methane with 64 kg of oxygen produces 44 kg of carbon dioxide and 36 kg of water. Which law does this illustrate?
explanation: Total mass of reactants equals total mass of products.

question: Nitrogen reacts with oxygen to form several different oxides. Which law explains this behavior?
description: Different compounds form in simple whole-number mass ratios.

question: A compound always contains the same elements combined in the same proportion by mass. Which law states this?
description: Compounds have fixed composition by mass.

question: Which set of compounds best demonstrates the law of multiple proportions?
description: Nitrogen oxides combine in simple whole-number ratios.

question: Which statement is NOT part of Dalton’s atomic theory?
options: Atoms of different elements are different
Atoms of the same element have different masses
description: Dalton believed atoms of the same element have identical mass.

question: Which was determined by Millikan’s oil-drop experiment?
What did Rutherford’s experiment reveal?
options: Mass and positive charge concentrated in nucleus

question: Which particles have nearly equal mass?
Which rays carry the same charge as helium nuclei?
Which particles are deflected toward the positive plate?
description: Beta particles are negatively charged electrons.

question: What did Rutherford’s gold foil experiment prove?
description: Most alpha particles passed straight through the foil.

question: Which atomic model is called the plum pudding model?
description: Thomson suggested electrons embedded in positive sphere.

question: Which model is called the planetary model?
Which model describes electrons using probability?
Region of high probability of finding an electron?
Correct historical order of atomic models?
